_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-8 Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit (Revised 3/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-8: Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit — MDHHS Approved 3/31/2025
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the distribution of naloxone leave-behind kits by EMS to patients, family members, or bystanders following an opioid-related emergency. Kits are provided to reduce the risk of death from future opioid overdose events. Kit contents are standardized per MDHHS requirements. EMS vehicles must carry 2 kits. Replacement must occur within 24 hours of distribution.

sections.title: Kit Contents
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-8: Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit — Contents
points: Required kit contents per MDHHS standard:
— Narcan (naloxone) 4 mg nasal spray — minimum 1 dose (2 doses preferred)
— Replacement form (for requesting kit replacement through MCA/agency)
— Local opioid resources form (community treatment and support resources)
— Optional: CPR face shield (MCA may include)
Kit must be stored intact and sealed; all contents must be present and not expired

sections.title: Distribution Criteria and Process
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-8: Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit — Distribution
points: Distribute naloxone leave-behind kit to any patient, family member, or bystander following an opioid overdose or suspected opioid use emergency
MCA Optional — only distribute if MCA has authorized leave-behind kit program
Offer kit to: patient (if alert and able to receive), family member or close contact present on scene, bystander who called or responded
Brief recipient on kit contents and basic nasal naloxone administration
Instruct recipient: call 911 first; administer naloxone; provide rescue breathing if trained; monitor for response; second dose in opposite nostril after 3 minutes if no response
Document kit distribution in PCR including: recipient (patient/family/bystander), time, and kit lot number

sections.title: Vehicle Stocking and Replacement
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-8: Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit — Stocking
points: EMS vehicles must carry 2 naloxone leave-behind kits when MCA has authorized the leave-behind program
Distributed kits must be replaced within 24 hours through agency supply or MCA-designated exchange
Kit expiration dates must be checked at each shift; expired kits must be replaced before deployment
Replacement documentation must be maintained per agency and MCA records requirements

mca_selections_required.item: Naloxone Leave-Behind Kit Program — MCA Authorization
mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize EMS agencies to carry and distribute naloxone leave-behind kits
options: Authorized — EMS agencies carry and distribute leave-behind kits per protocol
Not authorized — leave-behind kit program not active in this MCA

scope_notes: Kit contents: Narcan 4mg nasal spray, MDHHS safety card, replacement form, local resources form; optional CPR face shield
2 kits per vehicle when program authorized
Distribute to: patient, family member, or bystander after opioid emergency
Brief recipient on use; document in PCR: recipient, time, lot number
